message:
feat(providers): zero-copy OpenAI serialization, empty-base defaults, Gemini robustness

Three categories of provider improvements:

1. Zero-copy OpenAI request serialization:
   - OpenAIRequest, OpenAIMessage, OpenAIContent, OpenAITool now use
     lifetime-parameterized borrows (&'a str, Cow<'a, str>) instead of
     owned Strings, eliminating allocations on the serialization hot path.
   - build_request() and build_messages_with_role() propagate lifetimes
     from Context through to the wire format.
   - stream_options field is now None when include_usage is false (was
     always Some with include_usage=false, which some endpoints rejected).
   - Test call sites updated to satisfy borrow checker (extract context
     and options into locals before passing to build_request).

2. Empty/whitespace base URL handling:
   - normalize_anthropic_base, normalize_openai_base,
     normalize_openai_responses_base, and normalize_cohere_base now
     trim whitespace first and return the canonical default endpoint
     for empty/whitespace-only input instead of producing malformed URLs.
   - Unit tests added for empty-input cases in both providers/mod.rs
     and integration tests.

3. Gemini/Vertex streaming robustness:
   - Add GeminiPart::Unknown(Value) catch-all variant so new API part
     types (e.g. executableCode, codeExecutionResult) don't cause hard
     deserialization failures that terminate the stream.
   - Emit ThinkingEnd events for open thinking blocks on finish_reason
     (was only emitting TextEnd, leaving thinking blocks unclosed).
   - Map FUNCTION_CALL finish reason to StopReason::ToolUse in Gemini.
   - Azure provider: trim whitespace from base URL before normalization.
   - Vertex: same Unknown part handling and ThinkingEnd emission as Gemini.
   - Add test_stream_ignores_unknown_parts test for Vertex.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
